title: MDA feature importance: score by neg-log-loss/F1, not accuracy@0.5
source: LdP book audit (6 parallel read-only auditors vs AFML + Machine Learning for Asset Managers, books in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs), 2026-06-03. Conversation: 'audita el proyecto usando los libros'.
why: mda_importance scorer is accuracy_score at a hard 0.5 cut (run_xau_d1.py:396-397, run_multi_h4.py:755). AFML 8 (Snippet 8.3) and MLfAM Snippet 6.3 score MDA by NEGATIVE LOG-LOSS, and AFML explicitly warns that accuracy misleads on imbalanced meta-labels (AFML_book.txt:3514) -- exactly the documented <30-trade fold-starvation regime. neg_log_loss is ALREADY used for the hyperparam search (run_xau_d1.py:417) and was not reused for MDA. The 0.5 cut also ignores the per-fold inner-CV-selected threshold.
value_rationale: Cheap correctness fix; makes MDA rankings trustworthy and makes the (correctly plumbed) sample weights actually bite.
scope: Replace _mda_scorer accuracy@0.5 with -log_loss(y_true, y_prob, sample_weight=...) (or F1) in run_xau_d1.py and run_multi_h4.py
Permutation location is already correct (OOS test fold, purged WF) -- do not change that
Re-run ema_cross positive control; ranking should stay consistent but resolution improve
recommendation: Adopt neg-log-loss scorer to match AFML 8.3 / MLfAM 6.3.
out_of_scope: MDI (not used, correctly). Clustered importance (separate entry).

history.reason: LdP book audit: MDA uses accuracy@0.5 vs book neg-log-loss
Implemented: MDA scorer switched to neg_log_loss_scorer (AFML 8.3) in run_xau_d1 + run_multi_h4; reusable helper in feature_importance.py. Tests green.

test(regression): re-anchor Phase 1 baseline to post-audit-fix numbers (B0129-B0133)
